summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a simulation in probability?

Probability = (favorable outcomes) / (total outcomes). Working through the calculation gives A model that uses random devices to represent real-world situations. The other options — 'A mathematical proof of a theorem', 'An exact calculation of probability', and 'A diagram showing all possible outcomes' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the first step in designing a simulation?
